description: Clean domestic economy flight — should be compliant
receipt_text:
United Airlines
Date: 2025-04-14
Flight UA2341 ORD-DEN (Economy)
Base Fare: $287.00
Taxes & Fees: $43.50
Total: $330.50
notes: Economy domestic flight well under any threshold — TEP-005 §2.1

description: Solo dinner over $75 cap — should be rejected
receipt_text:
Alinea Chicago
Date: 2025-03-20
Dinner for 1
Food: $220.00
Tax: $22.00
Gratuity (18%): $39.60
Total: $281.60
notes: Chicago is Tier 2 — dinner cap is $75/person. $281.60 solo dinner far exceeds — not reimbursable per TEP-002 §2.2

receipt_text:
Franklin Barbecue Austin
Date: 2025-05-10
Brisket Plate: $22.00
Craft Beer x3: $24.00
Total: $46.00
notes: Alcohol on solo travel never reimbursable per TEP-003 §3.1 — rejected is correct

description: Hotel in NYC Tier 1 under $350 cap — should be compliant
receipt_text:
Marriott Midtown New York
Check-in: 2025-02-10  Check-out: 2025-02-12
Room Rate: $289.00/night x 2 nights
Occupancy Tax: $55.00
Total: $633.00
notes: NYC is Tier 1 cap $350/night. $289 base + $27.50 tax/night = $316.50/night — within cap. TEP-004 §3

description: Hotel in NYC exceeding $350 Tier 1 cap — should be rejected
receipt_text:
Four Seasons New York
Check-in: 2025-02-10  Check-out: 2025-02-12
Room Rate: $520.00/night x 2 nights
Occupancy Tax: $95.00
Total: $1135.00
notes: NYC Tier 1 cap is $350/night. $520 base far exceeds cap — not reimbursable per TEP-004 §3

description: First class airfare — should be rejected
receipt_text:
Delta Air Lines
Date: 2025-06-01
Flight DL204 JFK-LAX (First Class)
Base Fare: $2,850.00
Taxes & Fees: $68.00
Total: $2,918.00
notes: First class is never reimbursable regardless of grade or trip purpose. TEP-005 §2.4

description: Uber Black when UberX was available — should be flagged
receipt_text:
Uber
Date: 2025-03-15
Uber Black — O'Hare Airport to Marriott Downtown Chicago
Fare: $87.00
Total: $87.00
notes: Premium rideshare categories (Uber Black) not reimbursable unless only option. TEP-006 §2.2

description: Standard UberX airport transfer — should be compliant
receipt_text:
Uber
Date: 2025-04-10
UberX — Denver International Airport to Marriott Downtown
Fare: $34.00
Total: $34.00
notes: Standard UberX for airport transfer — fully reimbursable. TEP-006 §2.1

description: Business class on domestic flight — should be rejected
receipt_text:
American Airlines
Date: 2025-05-20
Flight AA100 BOS-LAX (Business Class)
Base Fare: $1,450.00
Taxes: $42.00
Total: $1,492.00
notes: Business class only permitted on international flights of 10+ hours with VP approval. Domestic business class is not reimbursable. TEP-005 §2.3

description: Client dinner under $150/person cap — should be compliant
receipt_text:
Gibson's Bar & Steakhouse Chicago
Date: 2025-04-22
Client Entertainment Dinner
Attendees: 2 Northwind employees + 2 external clients
Food: $380.00
Tax: $34.20
Gratuity (18%): $68.40
Total: $482.60
notes: $482.60 / 4 attendees = $120.65/person. Under $150/person client entertainment cap. TEP-002 §4.1

question: What is the per-meal cap for dinner on domestic solo travel?
notes: TEP-002 §2: Dinner cap is $75/person. Should answer precisely from POLICY1.

question: What is the current stock price of Northwind Logistics?
notes: Out of scope — not a policy topic.

question: Is alcohol reimbursable for business travel?
notes: TEP-003 §2: Reimbursable ONLY for sanctioned client entertainment with external attendees + VP approval + max $50/person. Should answer from POLICY1.

question: What is the nightly hotel cap for New York?
notes: TEP-004 §3: NYC is Tier 1 — $350/night. Should answer from POLICY1.

question: Can I fly business class on a long international flight?
notes: TEP-005 §2.3: Business class permitted on 10+ hour international segments with VP approval. Should answer from POLICY2.

question: What is the IRS mileage reimbursement rate for 2025?
notes: TEP-006 §4.1: $0.67 per mile. Should answer from POLICY2.

question: What approval is needed for international travel?
notes: TEP-013 §2.1: VP (Grade 9) approval required for all international travel. Should answer from POLICY4.

question: How do I reset my password?
notes: Out of scope — IT topic, not in any policy document.

question: What is the per-diem rate for a Tier 1 city like San Francisco?
notes: TEP-008 §3: Tier 1 per-diem is $90/day. Should answer from POLICY2.

question: What is the company vacation policy?
notes: Out of scope — HR/vacation policy not in any of the 8 documents.

question: What meals are covered by a Tier 2 per-diem and what is the daily rate?
notes: TEP-008 §3, §5: Tier 2 = $70/day covering all 3 meals + incidentals (excludes alcohol). Should answer from POLICY2.

question: Can I use Uber Black for airport transfers?
notes: TEP-006 §2.2: Premium categories not reimbursable unless only option. Should answer from POLICY2.
